findings.title: Missing Explicit Authentication and RBAC Implementation
findings.description: The plan lists inter-service authentication as a decision point but lacks concrete tasks to implement API gateways, JWT validation, or mTLS. Admin endpoints in the Catalog and sensitive Finance/RMS endpoints are currently unprotected in the implementation steps.
findings.recommendation: Add a dedicated task to implement an API Gateway or service mesh for mTLS, and enforce JWT/RBAC validation on all administrative and financial endpoints.

findings.title: Potential Secret Exposure in ConfigMaps
findings.description: Task 1005 creates a ConfigMap for database connection strings. If these URLs contain embedded credentials, they will be exposed in plain text within the cluster.
findings.recommendation: Store database credentials in Kubernetes Secrets and inject them as environment variables, constructing the final connection string within the application code rather than storing the full URL in a ConfigMap.

findings.title: AI Agent Prompt Injection Risks to Backend APIs
findings.description: The AI Agent translates natural language into backend API calls via MCP tools. Without strict input validation on the backend services, prompt injection attacks could allow users to manipulate parameters sent to the RMS or Finance services.
findings.recommendation: Implement rigorous input validation and sanitization on all backend API endpoints, treating the AI Agent as an untrusted client.

findings.title: Lack of Encryption at Rest for Sensitive Data
findings.description: The Finance and Vetting services handle sensitive business data, but there are no tasks for field-level encryption or encryption at rest for the PostgreSQL databases.
findings.recommendation: Update the PostgreSQL provisioning task to enable encryption at rest and implement field-level encryption for highly sensitive PII/financial data in the application layer.

findings.title: Missing Automated Vulnerability Scanning
findings.description: While dependency versions are pinned, there are no tasks for automated vulnerability scanning of dependencies or container images before deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add a task to integrate container image scanning and dependency auditing into the CI/CD pipeline.

summary: The current task plan establishes a solid foundation with early considerations for network policies, rate limiting, and secure secret storage. However, critical gaps exist in the explicit implementation of authentication across microservices, and the AI agent introduces novel prompt injection risks that must be mitigated. Addressing these authentication, secret management, and input validation concerns is paramount to securing the platform before production deployment.
strengths: Network policies are considered from the start and hardened in scale tasks.
TLS certificate management is explicitly planned for production.
S3 credentials are appropriately stored in Kubernetes Secrets.
Rate limiting is included in the Catalog service to prevent abuse.
